format.music: soft ambient pad, low volume bed

scenes.narration: Placing an order? Use the same five steps every time. Lab, med, imaging, referral — same shape.
scenes.motion: fade in title, soft scale 1.0 → 1.02, hold
scenes.callout: subtitle pill: Lab · Med · Imaging · Referral

scenes.narration: One. Look at the top of the chart. Check the patient name and the encounter you're in. Wrong encounter is the number one reason orders land in the wrong place.
scenes.motion: pan from name → encounter selector, 1.5s
scenes.callout: highlight ring around encounter selector, red pulse 2x

scenes.narration: Two. In the chart sidebar, click Orders or Order Entry. If you don't see it, your role or department context is off — fix that first.
scenes.motion: cursor moves to sidebar, click ripple on Orders
scenes.callout: side note: 'No Orders? Check role + department'

scenes.narration: Three. Pick the order type, then search by name and select the exact orderable.
scenes.motion: tiles fade in in sequence, search field types 'CBC' as example placeholder

scenes.narration: Four. Check the details panel. Priority, frequency, dose, indication. Anything starred or red is required before you can sign.
scenes.motion: scroll detail panel slowly, stars glow yellow

scenes.narration: Five. Click Sign. If you're not the responsible provider, route for cosign instead.
scenes.motion: button press, success check fades in

scenes.narration: If it worked, the order moves to Active and shows up on the order list.
scenes.motion: new row slides in, green status pill

scenes.narration: If sign fails, read the inline message — it names the missing field. Fill it in and sign again.
scenes.motion: shake on Sign, error banner slides under field
scenes.callout: arrow pointing to inline error text

scenes.narration: Still stuck after two tries? Capture your role, the patient encounter, the order name, and the exact error text, then send it to your support contact.
scenes.motion: card assembles field-by-field
scenes.callout: checklist ticks for each captured field

title: Notes — where to write and sign

format.music: soft ambient pad, low volume bed

scenes.narration: Writing a note? Use the same five steps every time. Same shape as orders.
scenes.motion: fade in title, subtle scale
scenes.callout: subtitle pill: same shape as Orders

scenes.narration: One. Check the patient name and the encounter at the top of the chart. The note will attach to whatever encounter is open right now.
scenes.motion: pan to encounter selector, ring pulse
scenes.callout: warning chip: 'Note attaches to open encounter'

scenes.narration: Two. In the chart sidebar, click Notes or Documentation. If Notes is missing, your role or department context is off — fix that first.
scenes.motion: cursor click ripple on Notes item

scenes.narration: Three. Pick the note type. Progress, H and P, procedure, discharge. The note type controls which template and required fields you'll see.
scenes.motion: grid tiles fade in, hover state on Progress

scenes.narration: Four. Fill in every starred section. The editor will not let you sign until they're done.
scenes.motion: scroll editor, stars glow yellow on empty sections

scenes.narration: Five. Click Sign. If a cosign is needed, route it to the right provider.
scenes.motion: button press, success check fades in

scenes.narration: If it worked, the note moves to Signed and shows on the chart.
scenes.motion: new row slides in, Signed pill

scenes.narration: If sign fails, read the inline error — it names the exact missing field. Fill it in and try again.
scenes.motion: shake on Sign, error banner slides under section
scenes.callout: arrow pointing to inline error text

scenes.narration: Still stuck after two tries? Capture your role, the encounter, the note type, and the exact error text, then send it to your support contact.
scenes.motion: card assembles field-by-field
scenes.callout: checklist ticks for each captured field

format.music: soft ambient pad, low volume bed

scenes.narration: A SmartLink came up blank, or pulled the wrong info? Work it in four quick checks before you escalate.
scenes.motion: fade in title, subtitle pill slides in

scenes.narration: One. Patient and encounter. Check the top of the chart. SmartLinks pull from the chart you're in — wrong chart, wrong data.
scenes.motion: pan up to banner, ring pulse on encounter

scenes.narration: Two. Refresh the note, or delete the SmartLink and insert it again. A stale insert won't pick up new data until it re-resolves.
scenes.motion: token shown blank → delete → re-insert → resolves with text
scenes.callout: arrow loop icon over the token

scenes.narration: Three. Look for a prompt. Some SmartLinks need a date range, a result type, or a user pick before they return anything.
scenes.motion: dialog slides up, starred field glows

scenes.narration: Four. If it's a shared template or shared SmartTool and it's still blank, this is a build issue, not a click issue. That means it needs the template owner.
scenes.motion: template card highlights, arrow routes to owner avatar

scenes.narration: If one of the first three checks fixed it, you're done.
scenes.motion: green check fades in over token

scenes.narration: If you reach check four, capture the SmartLink name, the patient encounter, and a screenshot, then route it to the documentation or template owner.
scenes.motion: card assembles field-by-field
scenes.callout: checklist ticks for each captured field
